q: What structural feature distinguishes arteries from veins?
options: Arteries have thinner walls and larger lumens
Arteries lack a tunica externa layer
Arteries have a thicker tunica media with more smooth muscle and elastic fibers
Arteries always carry oxygenated blood while veins carry deoxygenated blood
explanation: The thick tunica media of arteries contains smooth muscle and elastic fibers to withstand and dampen the high-pressure surges of blood from the heart.
